_meta.purpose: NAIC private-passenger-auto market share, used as the cross-carrier weight for F-bar(state) in apply_filed_changes.py (Layer B) WHEN per-filing SERFF written_premium is unavailable.
_meta.why: TX filings come from the TDI/Socrata open-data API (data.texas.gov), which carries NO written premium or policyholder count, so every TX row has written_premium=null. Without a weight, TX's state-level drift falls back to an EQUAL-weighted mean of carrier F's -- i.e. a 1.3M-book State Farm cut would count the same as a tiny regional. Market share supplies the correct economic weight where the SERFF jacket premium is missing.
_meta.unit: percent of national PPA direct premiums written
sources: NAIC 2024 Private Passenger Auto market share, top-25 by direct premiums written -- via Agency Checklists (published 2025-03-17): https://agencychecklists.com/2025/03/17/naic-2025-market-share-top-25-private-passenger-auto-insurers-74894/
Cross-checked against ValuePenguin 'Largest Auto Insurance Companies' (NAIC + S&P Global, refreshed 2026-07): https://www.valuepenguin.com/largest-auto-insurance-companies
_meta.keying: Keys are STATE_CARRIER_ADJ roster names, NOT NAIC group names. Mapped at build time: NAIC 'Berkshire Hathaway' -> GEICO; 'Auto Owners'/'Mercury'/'American Family'/'Auto Club' etc. are NOT in the ADJ roster so omitted. Safeco shares Liberty Mutual's group figure (same NAIC group) -- if both ever file the same state's drift the group is double-counted; add a per-state override then.
_meta.floor_note: A participating carrier absent from this table (small regionals below the NAIC top-25: Root, Donegal, Germania, National General [rolls up under the Allstate group in NAIC reporting], and individual state Farm Bureaus) gets the floor: a small, non-zero, correctly-de-weighted influence. 0.20 sits just under the top-25 tail (NJM 0.43).
_meta.per_state_limitation: These are NATIONAL shares. A regional that is top-5 IN a specific state but small nationally (Texas Farm Bureau in TX, Tennessee Farm Bureau in TN) is under-weighted by its national number. Add states[ST][carrier]=<sourced share> if/when such a carrier has a POST-ANCHOR filing that participates in that state's drift. As of 2026-07-06 none do (TX Farm Bureau has no in-window filing; TN Farm Bureau's filing is pre-anchor).
_meta.consumed_by: apply_filed_changes.py (Layer B weight fallback). Also available for future tracker credibility copy ('carriers representing N% of the market').
